title: Phage stock preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"962pc","text":"A protocol for phage lambda and phage T7 phage stock preparation","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Grow E. coli bacterial host (for example LE392, DH10B or DH5alpha) in LB medium overnight at 37 °C
Prepare and autoclave 1 Molarity (M) CaCl2 and 1 Molarity (M) MgCl2
Add 50 µL of 1 Molarity (M) CaCl2 and 50 µL of 1 Molarity (M) MgCl2 to 50 mL of LB medium. Make aliquots of 10 mL and inoculate with 0.1 volumes of overnight bacterial host.
Add 100 µL of high titer phage lysate (> 10 8 PFU/ml).
Incubate at 37 °C with agitation for ∼ 05:00:00 or until lysate clears.
Collect phage lysate and store at 4 °C until clean up.
Pool phage lysate into 50 mL sterile falcon centrifuge tubes and centrifuge at 4,000 rpm for 00:25:00 .
Filter-sterilize the phage supernatant using a 0.22 μm filter to yield a bacterial cell-free phage lysate.
Determine phage titer using plaque or spot assay.
creator: Marijn Ceelen
